hypothesis: If large language models (particularly reasoning-optimized models like o4-mini) are prompted with structured in-context examples of increasing complexity, then they will achieve higher precision, recall, and F1-scores in extracting PROV-DM-compliant provenance graphs from materials synthesis literature compared to smaller or non-reasoning models and zero-shot prompting, because more capable models combined with demonstration examples better capture the structured relational and parametric information required for accurate graph construction.
